_researchNotes: GigSalad lead notifications come from leads@gigsalad.com (help.gigsalad.com/article/62-not-getting-gig-lead-emails lists leads@/gigs@/yourfriends@/noreply@gigsalad.com as whitelist addresses; Gmail prompt quoted as 'Do this for future messages from leads@gigsalad.com?'). GigSalad deliberately withholds client contact details: 'GigSalad does not provide client email addresses directly... phone numbers... available to paid members after a response has been sent' (help.gigsalad.com/article/64-client-contact-information). The notification body is intentionally light — 'The email will not have the lead's details, so you'll need to click the button in the email to see the event information' (help.gigsalad.com/article/81-email-notifications) — but the richer variant (sent when 'the event planner has provided additional details', same article) carries a summary sentence plus labeled event lines (Event Date / Location / Number of Guests / Budget), the same field set Mailparser's line-label workflow targets ('Name: {{LeadName}}' style, one field per line). Client identified by first name + last initial only; no email/phone ever present. Exact subject wording is not publicly documented; this fixture uses the event-type-plus-location subject shape vendors report.
subject: New lead: Wedding Reception in Austin, TX
textBody:
Hi Demo DJ Co.,

You have a new lead on GigSalad!

Marisol G. is looking for a DJ for a Wedding Reception.

Event Type: Wedding Reception
Event Date: Saturday, June 14, 2026
Location: Austin, TX
Number of Guests: 120
Budget: $800 - $1,200

View the full event information and respond to the client:
https://www.gigsalad.com/leads/view/abc123

Respond quickly! You have a 25% better chance of booking if you respond in less than an hour.

Good luck,
The GigSalad Team
